steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 6107 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit nested location-area encounter relations and preserve filtered provenance under budgets; no user-visible meaning may rely on color, cursor motion, or pointer input; then change one compiler, Node runtime, emitted artifact, package, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.why: Retrieving Pokedex Ts Explore Changed Fixtures through the concrete validated provenance-rich encounter explorer case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test empty, malformed nested, duplicate, multi-version, filtered, large, aborted, cached, and changed-area payloads.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Explore Changed Fixtures precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A lab TypeScript team handles case 5532 during an out-of-order Promise fault rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit nested location-area encounter relations and preserve filtered provenance under budgets; unknown external values must not cross the runtime validator; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test empty, malformed nested, duplicate, multi-version, filtered, large, aborted, cached, and changed-area payloads.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One complete popular location fixture represents the endpoint contract.
